title: Scale PostgreSQL to 3-node HA cluster with connection pooling and automated backups
subtasks: Update CloudNative-PG Cluster CR to set replicas/instances to 3 for HA.
Configure synchronous replication with at least one synchronous standby.
Add pod anti-affinity rules to ensure PostgreSQL pods are distributed across nodes/zones.
Enable PgBouncer connection pooling (either as a sidecar or separate deployment) and configure pool size for production workloads.
Configure daily automated backups and enable WAL archiving to S3/R2 (set up S3 bucket, credentials, and retention policy).
Test failover by simulating primary pod failure and verify automatic failover and application reconnection.
Document backup/restore and failover procedures.

title: Scale Redis to 3-node HA cluster with Sentinel and production memory policy
subtasks: Update Redis/Valkey operator CR to enable Redis Sentinel mode with 3 Redis pods and 3 Sentinel pods.
Configure pod anti-affinity to distribute Redis and Sentinel pods across nodes.
Set maxmemory and maxmemory-policy (e.g., allkeys-lru) for production cache eviction.
Enable persistence (AOF or RDB) as appropriate for cache durability requirements.
Test Sentinel failover by deleting the master pod and verifying automatic promotion and client reconnection.
Document Sentinel endpoints and failover process.

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN and TLS for static assets and public endpoints
subtasks: Ensure all public domains are proxied through Cloudflare (orange cloud enabled).
Set SSL/TLS mode to 'Full (Strict)' in Cloudflare dashboard.
Configure cache rules for static asset paths (e.g., /static/*, /_next/static/*, images, fonts, CSS, JS) with long TTLs.
Set cache-control headers in application responses for static assets.
Enable HSTS and Brotli compression in Cloudflare.
Configure page rules to bypass cache for API endpoints and dynamic routes.
Test CDN cache hits and SSL enforcement on all public endpoints.

title: Set up Cloudflare Tunnel ingress for Morgan and web endpoints
subtasks: Create and configure Cloudflare Tunnel for all public endpoints (Morgan agent, web frontend, etc.).
Store tunnel credentials as a Kubernetes Secret.
Deploy cloudflared as a Deployment in the cluster with credentials mounted.
Configure ingress rules mapping public hostnames to internal Kubernetes services.
Verify DNS CNAME records point to the tunnel's .cfargotunnel.com address.
Test tunnel health and public endpoint accessibility.

title: Apply Kubernetes network policies to restrict pod-to-pod and egress traffic
subtasks: Audit service communication paths and document required traffic flows.
Apply default-deny NetworkPolicy for each namespace.
Create allow policies for required service-to-service communication (e.g., API to PostgreSQL/Redis, web to API, cloudflared to web/Morgan).
Allow DNS egress and required external API endpoints (OpenAI, Cloudflare, etc.).
Label pods/namespaces for policy selectors.
Test allowed and denied traffic paths using test pods.

title: Update all service manifests with readiness/liveness probes and resource limits
subtasks: For each Deployment/StatefulSet, add or update readinessProbe and livenessProbe (HTTP GET to /healthz or /ready).
Add startupProbe for services with slow initialization.
Set resource requests and limits for CPU and memory based on production sizing.
Apply updated manifests and verify all pods are healthy and probes pass.
Test pod restarts and verify readiness/liveness behavior.

title: Configure OpenTelemetry collector and Jaeger for production observability
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector with production sampling rates (e.g., 1-10% for high-traffic endpoints, 100% for errors).
Configure exporters to Jaeger and/or external observability backends.
Deploy Jaeger with persistent storage (e.g., PVC or external DB) for trace retention.
Update application deployments to export traces to the collector endpoint.
Verify traces are collected, persisted, and queryable in Jaeger.
Document trace retention and sampling configuration.

title: Define and apply alert rules for SLO violations and critical infrastructure events
subtasks: Define SLOs for key services (e.g., API latency, error rate, availability).
Create Prometheus alert rules for SLO violations (e.g., 5xx error rate >1% for 5m, p95 latency >1s).
Add alerts for infrastructure health (PostgreSQL failover, Redis failover, cloudflared tunnel down, pod restarts).
Configure alert delivery to on-call channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty, etc.).
Test alert firing and notification delivery.
